C:
Done. Code execution got declined by the permission prompt, so I verified by tracing rather than running it — here's the fix:

```js
function lastIndexOf(arr, x){ for (let i = arr.length - 1; i >= 0; i--){ if (arr[i] === x) return i; } return -1; }
```

The only change is the loop direction: it now scans from `arr.length - 1` down to `0`, so the first match it hits is the *last* occurrence, and it returns `-1` when nothing matches.

Trace check on `[1, 2, 3, 2, 1]` with `x = 2`: i=4 is `1` (skip), i=3 is `2` → returns `3`, which is the last index of `2`. Your original returned `1` (the first). Edge cases hold too: an empty array never enters the loop and returns `-1`, and a single-element match returns `0`.

Note this function lives only inside the eval prompt string in `run-check-ab.mjs:71`, not as standalone source — so there was no project file to edit; the corrected version above is the deliverable.
